prompt: Implement a Residential Life Operations Portal for offline student housing programs to manage resident profiles, housing agreements, and service communications in a single on-prem experience. The primary roles are Housing Administrator (full configuration and data governance), Residence Staff (front-desk and case handling), and Student (self-service view and messaging). The React-based web UI provides a resident directory with fast search and filters by building, class year, and move-in status, plus guided forms to create or update student basics, enrollment/department/class mapping, emergency contacts, and move-in records. Staff can upload agreement attachments (PDF/JPG/PNG up to 15 MB) and see inline validation feedback (missing required fields, invalid US phone format like 555-123-4567, duplicate candidates). Bulk import/export supports CSV with a preview step that flags row-level errors and allows a merge duplicates workflow when a student appears multiple times (match on student ID, then secondary match on name + date of birth). A built-in messaging center enables session-based chat between staff and students with quick replies, optional image sharing, and clear interaction cues including sent/delivered/read status across multiple logged-in devices; students can block a staff member from initiating new chats, while staff can still send system notices. System notifications are templated for operational events such as hire/onboarding tasks for student workers, appointment reschedules, settlement outcomes, and arbitration decisions, and appear in an inbox with priority labels and required-read acknowledgments for critical items. Configuration screens let administrators set booking and visit rules such as a 14-day rolling booking window, same-day cutoff at 5:00 PM, holiday blackout dates, and no-show policies that temporarily restrict bookings after 2 no-shows within 30 days, with canary rollout to 10% of buildings before full enablement. The backend uses Spring Boot to enforce business rules, validate inputs, and expose decoupled REST APIs consumed by the React frontend, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for students, housing contracts, message threads, notifications, and configuration versions. Authentication is strictly offline and local: username plus password only, with passwords hashed using BCrypt (work factor 12), account lockout after 5 failed attempts in 15 minutes, optional admin-initiated disable/freeze, and blacklist management for policy violations; account deletion performs a 30-day soft delete with irreversible purge afterward, while preserving required audit logs. All sensitive fields (date of birth, emergency contact details) are encrypted at rest using AES-256, and the UI masks them by default unless the user has an authorized role. Open interfaces are supported via locally hosted REST endpoints and webhooks intended for on-prem third-party devices (door controllers, hardware terminals, local notification boards), protected with HMAC-signed requests, timestamp-based replay protection (reject if skew exceeds 5 minutes), and rate limiting (for example, 60 requests/minute per integration key). A configurable multi-source data collection engine runs scheduled crawl tasks against locally reachable sites or intranet pages, configurable by site, city, keyword, and pagination rules, with incremental updates, resumable checkpoints, task-level throttles, and concurrency caps (default 5 parallel fetchers) to protect internal resources. Analytics dashboards compute booking conversion, no-show rate, slot utilization, and settlement outcome completion from stored events; metrics refresh on a 15-minute schedule and remain fully functional without internet access, with all integrations and notifications constrained to local network delivery only.
